channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Improving the Markdown Editor for Work Items
item.description: We introduced the Markdown editor in July 2025 to bring Markdown support to large text fields in work items. Since then, we’ve received valuable customer feedback highlighting challenges with the editing experience, particularly when switching in and out of edit mode. Many users found the current...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise with data residency
item.description: GitHub Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. You can create secure, configurable, instantly available cloud development environments while maintaining strict compliance with your data… The post Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.114
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.114 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student plan via Copilot auto model selection. This model is part of Auto in GitHub Copilot Chat on Visual Studio Code, Visual… The post GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs
item.description: GitHub Mobile now makes it easier to manage agentic workflows on the go with a refreshed Copilot tab, native session logs, and more in-app controls for agent sessions. On Android,… The post GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues
item.description: GitHub Mobile now makes it easier to delegate work with a new Assign an Agent option in the issue overflow menu, so you can get started more quickly. When assigning… The post GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 3
item.description: Vectors, Analytics, and the End of ETL Part 3 of 4 – The Multimodal Database Series In Part 1: The Polyglot Tax we laid out the fraud detection scenario: a transaction comes in, and before you approve or deny it you need five checks. Order history (relational). Device fingerprint (JSON)....
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI
item.description: /fleet lets Copilot CLI dispatch multiple agents in parallel. Learn how to write prompts that split work across files, declare dependencies, and avoid common pitfalls. The post Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Matt Nigh

item.title: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description: aka, yes your inbox can have a robot in it now
item.dc:creator: Alex Moore

item.title: Bookmark Studio: evolving bookmarks in Visual Studio
item.description: Bookmarks in Visual Studio have always been a simple, reliable feature. Many developers use them regularly, and over the years we’ve heard consistent feedback from those users. Bookmarks were useful, but there were a few core gaps that kept them from being as effective and relevant as they could...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: v1.15.0-beta2
item.description: 1.15.0-beta2 (April 01, 2026) NEW FEATURES: We now produce builds for Windows ARM64 (#32719) You can set a deprecated attribute on variable and output blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. This will produce warnings when passing in a value for a deprecated variable or when referencing a...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Project Nighthawk: A Research Agent Built for Field Engineering
item.description: If you work in field engineering, you know the scenario. A customer is deploying AKS in a regulated environment. They hit an issue during node bootstrapping. They want to know exactly what happens when a node joins the cluster, which components run in which order, and whether the behaviour they’re...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Upcoming deprecation of Claude Sonnet 4 in GitHub Copilot
item.description: We will deprecate the following model across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on 5/1/2026: Model Deprecation date Suggested alternative… The post Upcoming deprecation of Claude Sonnet 4 in GitHub Copilot appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Building with Azure Skills
item.description: Part 3 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: How to Install the Azure Plugin You’ve installed the Azure Skills Plugin. The Azure MCP server is running. You have a huge collection of tools and skills at your disposal. So, now what do you actually say to it? This post is a prompt cookbook....
item.dc:creator: Chris Harris

item.title: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description: Import data from external sources (Preview) – S3, Snowflake, Azure SQL Db along with external Data Connections (Preview) in Azure Machine Learning are being deprecated. These features will no longer be available after September 30, 2026. What this means
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over… The post Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DevOps March 2026 updates
item.description: The following updates were made to Azure DevOps in March 2026. The Remote MCP Server entered public preview, enabling seamless AI-powered integration with Azure DevOps without requiring a local server. Security improvements included
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid offers a variety of features in Public Preview – intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Public Preview Features: Cross-Tenant Webhook Delivery: Enables secu
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Dependabot now supports Xcode projects using SwiftPM with .xcodeproj manifests
item.description: Dependabot can now detect and update Swift package dependencies in Xcode projects that manage packages through .xcodeproj bundles, even when no Package.swift file is present. This improvement has been one… The post Dependabot now supports Xcode projects using SwiftPM with .xcodeproj manifests...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues is now generally available. User delegation SAS allows users to create a more secure SAS token than account or service SAS by tying the SAS token to the delegator. This extension of UDK
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid now offers a variety of new features intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Generally Available features: MQTT Message Ordering: Ensures in-order message de
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure now available from new cloud region in Denmark
item.description: Microsoft announces the opening of its cloud region in Denmark to accelerate digital transformation and AI innovation. The Denmark East region provides Danish customers with local, secure cloud infrastructure, supporting local data residency, low latency,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GitHub secret scanning — coverage update
item.description: GitHub secret scanning continually updates its detectors, validators, and analyzers. Here’s what’s new. Nine new secret detectors from seven providers, including Langchain, Salesforce, and Figma. Secrets from Figma, Google, OpenVSX,… The post GitHub secret scanning — coverage update appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings powerful new dashboard features for exporting and importing telemetry, querying telemetry via API, and improved GenAI telemetry visualization. The post Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: James Newton-King

item.title: The threat to critical infrastructure has changed. Has your readiness?
item.description: Five facts critical infrastructure (CI) leaders need to act on in 2026, grounded in what Microsoft Threat Intelligence is observing across sectors right now. The post The threat to critical infrastructure has changed. Has your readiness? appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sherrod DeGrippo

item.title: Retirement: Manually registered Azure VPN Clients for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds will be retired on March 31, 2029
item.description: On March 31, 2029, we’ll be retiring manually registered Azure VPN clients for point-to-site connections with Microsoft Entra ID authentication for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds. Recommended action: To prevent disrupt
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in US Gov Arizona (a region without Availability Zone). Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional p
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Visual Studio March Update – Build Your Own Custom Agents
item.description: This month’s Visual Studio update gives you new ways to customize GitHub Copilot. Custom agents allow you to build specialized Copilot agents tailored to your team’s workflow, backed by the tools and knowledge sources that matter to your project. Alongside that, agent skills bring reusable...
item.dc:creator: Mark Downie

item.title: Agent-driven development in Copilot Applied Science
item.description: I used coding agents to build agents that automated part of my job. Here's what I learned about working better with coding agents. The post Agent-driven development in Copilot Applied Science appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Tyler McGoffin

item.title: Applying security fundamentals to AI: Practical advice for CISOs
item.description: Read actionable advice for CISOs on securing AI, managing risk, and applying core security principles in today’s AI‑powered environment. The post Applying security fundamentals to AI: Practical advice for CISOs appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Yonatan Zunger

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.0 adds Swift 6.2.4 support
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.0, which upgrades Swift analysis to 6.2.4,… The post CodeQL 2.25.0 adds Swift 6.2.4 support appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: WhatsApp malware campaign delivers VBScript and MSI backdoors
item.description: A malware campaign uses WhatsApp messages to deliver VBS scripts that initiate a multi-stage infection chain. The attack leverages renamed Windows tools and cloud-hosted payloads to install MSI backdoors and maintain persistent access to compromised systems. The post WhatsApp malware campaign...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: CodeQL pull requests insights on security overview now cover all protected branches
item.description: The CodeQL pull request insights tab in GitHub security overview now reports Copilot Autofix and alert statistics from all protected branches, not just the default branch. This gives you a… The post CodeQL pull requests insights on security overview now cover all protected branches appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd) – March 2026: Run and Debug AI Agents Locally, GitHub Copilot Integration, & Container App Jobs
item.description: Run, invoke, and monitor AI agents locally or in Microsoft Foundry with the new azd AI agent extension commands. Plus GitHub Copilot-powered project setup, Container App Jobs deployment, local preflight validation, and configurable timeouts. The post Azure Developer CLI (azd) – March 2026: Run and...
item.dc:creator: PuiChee (PC) Chan

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching for VM/VMSS
item.description: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching is now available in public preview for Azure VM/VMSS, bringing significantly faster and more reliable OS disk performance to your workloads. By caching the entire OS disk image on local VM storage (cache disk, resource
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Indonesia Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in the Indonesia Central Azure region, expanding regional availability for customers running mission‑critical OpenShift workloads on Azure. This release enables organizations in Indonesia and the br
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Deprecation of sidecar for remote-write for Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus
item.description: On March 31, 2027 Azure Monitor will be deprecating the sidecar for remote-write of Prometheus metrics to Azure Monitor Workspace. This is as part of our ongoing efforts to improve reliability and reduce complexity.We recommend configuring your self-hoste
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description: On March 31, 2029, Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired, and you'll need to migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster by that date. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster delivers the same distributed PostgreSQL capabilitie
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in South India (a region without Availability Zone).Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional price
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub security
item.description: Learn how to secure your projects and keep them safe with GitHub Advanced Security. The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub security appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kedasha Kerr

item.title: Addressing the OWASP Top 10 Risks in Agentic AI with Microsoft Copilot Studio
item.description: Agentic AI introduces new security risks. Learn how the OWASP Top 10 Risks for Agentic Applications maps to real mitigations in Microsoft Copilot Studio. The post Addressing the OWASP Top 10 Risks in Agentic AI with Microsoft Copilot Studio appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Efim Hudis

item.title: Writing Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure
item.description: This post shows how to write Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure. The post Writing Azure service-related unit tests with Docker using Spring Cloud Azure appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rujun Chen

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36 Sneak Peek
item.description: Kubernetes v1.36 is coming at the end of April 2026. This release will include removals and deprecations, and it is packed with an impressive number of enhancements. Here are some of the features we are most excited about in this cycle! Please note that this information reflects the current state...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How Microsoft Defender protects high-value assets in real-world attack scenarios
item.description: High-value assets including domain controllers, web servers, and identity infrastructure are frequent targets in sophisticated attacks. Microsoft Defender applies asset-aware protection using Microsoft Security Exposure Management to detect and block threats against these critical systems. This...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Azure SDK Release (March 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (March 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ronnie Geraghty

item.title: TypeScript AppHost in Aspire 13.2: same app model, different syntax
item.description: Aspire 13.2 enables TypeScript developers to define AppHosts directly in TypeScript, with the same streamlined Aspire application model. The post TypeScript AppHost in Aspire 13.2: same app model, different syntax appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sebastien Ros

item.title: What’s coming to our GitHub Actions 2026 security roadmap
item.description: A look at GitHub Actions’ 2026 roadmap, outlining how secure defaults, policy controls, and CI/CD observability harden the software supply chain end to end. The post What’s coming to our GitHub Actions 2026 security roadmap appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Greg Ose

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-March 2026
item.description: In late-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Configure built-in SQL code analysis rules and severity settings without editing project XML. Use Fabric connectivity and provisioning options to connect wi
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: A year of open source vulnerability trends: CVEs, advisories, and malware
item.description: Reviewed advisories hit a four-year low, malware advisories surged, and CNA publishing grew—here’s what changed and what it means for your triage and response. The post A year of open source vulnerability trends: CVEs, advisories, and malware appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Evans

item.title: Building Search-Enabled Agents with Azure AI Foundry and Semantic Kernel and A2A
item.description: A step-by-step guide to enable search in the semantic kernel agents using bing grounding in Azure Foundry AI Agent using A2A. The post Building Search-Enabled Agents with Azure AI Foundry and Semantic Kernel and A2A appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Munish Malhotra
